Title: Verify Welcome and all three messages for CSV-enrolled MDN (AU), then re-enrollment with same MDN
Action:
1. Create a new SMS contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=true, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique subscriberId and MDN.
2. Create the CSV file containing this contract/MDN in the format required by the system for enrollment.
3. Upload the CSV file to the configured S3 bucket path used for enrollment.
4. Wait for the enrollment job to run then wait up to ten minutes for processing to complete.
5. In the carrier enrollment database, run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target mt ORDER BY created_datetime DESC; Locate the row for the test MDN.
6. In the customer contracts database, run: SELECT * FROM customer_contracts WHERE mdn = '<your_mdn>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Replace <your_mdn> with the MDN used in Step 1. Note the contract_id.
7. Wait 20 seconds (20000 ms).
8. In Titan DB, verify status SUCCESS for the contract. Run: SELECT contract_id, status, created_datetime FROM <titan_contract_status_table> WHERE contract_id = '<contract_id>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC;
9. In ISS DB, verify the Welcome message for the new user. Run: SELECT * FROM sms_system.message ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 100;
10. Wait 90 seconds (90000 ms).
11. In Titan DB, get the message history ID for this contract. Run: SELECT * FROM titan_views.sms_system_message_realtime_view WHERE contract_id = '<contract_id>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10;
12. In Titan DB, verify all three message requests (Welcome, Remind1, Remind2) for this contract. Run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target WHERE contract_id = '<contract_id>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC;
13. Using the applicable admin or API process delete the MDN that was created in Step 1.
14. Using the applicable process re-add the same MDN (same phone number) for SMS.
15. Create a new CSV file containing the same MDN and the required contract fields. Upload this CSV to the S3 bucket.
16. Wait for the enrollment job to run then wait up to ten minutes for processing.
17. In the carrier enrollment database, run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target mt ORDER BY created_datetime DESC;
18. In the customer contracts database, run: SELECT * FROM customer_contracts WHERE mdn = '<same_mdn>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Note the contract_id.
19. Wait 5 seconds (5000 ms).
20. In Titan DB verify status CONTRACT_TRANSITIONED for the re-enrolled contract. Same query pattern as Step 8 with the re-enrolled contract_id.
21. In ISS DB verify the Welcome message is delivered again for the re-enrolled user. Same query as Step 9.
22. Wait 5 seconds (5000 ms).
23. In Titan DB get the message history ID for the re-enrolled contract. Same query pattern as Step 11.
24. In Titan DB verify all three message requests for the re-enrolled contract. Same query pattern as Step 12.
Expected Result:
1. Contract data ready.
2. CSV file created.
3. Upload successful.
4. Enrollment completes.
5. New enrollment record visible for the MDN.
6. Contract ID visible; contract_id noted.
7. Time for downstream processing.
8. Status = SUCCESS.
9. Welcome message present for the contract.
10. Time for message delivery.
11. Message history ID obtained.
12. All three message requests present/sent.
13. MDN deleted.
14. Same MDN re-added.
15. CSV created and uploaded.
16. Re-enrollment completes.
17. Re-enrollment record visible.
18. Contract ID for re-enrolled MDN visible.
19. Time for status update.
20. Status = CONTRACT_TRANSITIONED.
21. Welcome message present for re-enrolled contract.
22. Time for messages.
23. Message history ID obtained.
24. All three message requests present/sent.
Expected Result (Overall): First enrollment: SUCCESS, Welcome delivered, all three messages sent. Re-enrollment (same MDN): CONTRACT_TRANSITIONED, Welcome delivered again, all three messages sent again.

Title: Verify Reminder One message is delivered to the newly subscribed user after CSV enrollment (AU).
Action:
1. Create a new SMS contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=true, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique subscriberId and MDN.
2. Create the CSV file containing this contract/MDN in the format required by the system for enrollment.
3. Upload the CSV file to the configured S3 bucket path used for enrollment.
4. Wait for the enrollment job to run then wait up to ten minutes for processing to complete.
5. In the carrier enrollment database, run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target mt ORDER BY created_datetime DESC; Locate the row for the test MDN.
6. In the customer contracts database, run: SELECT * FROM customer_contracts WHERE mdn = '<mdn>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Replace <mdn> with the MDN from Step 1. Note the contract_id.
7. Wait 90 seconds (90000 ms) to allow Reminder One to be sent.
8. In ISS DB, verify the Reminder One message for the newly subscribed user. Run: SELECT * FROM sms_system.message ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 100; Filter or identify the row for this contract/user and message type Reminder One.
Expected Result:
1. Contract data ready.
2. CSV file created.
3. Upload successful.
4. Enrollment completes.
5. New enrollment record visible.
6. Contract ID visible.
7. Wait complete.
8. Reminder One message delivered and visible in ISS DB.
Expected Result (Overall): Reminder One message is delivered to the newly subscribed user (ISS DB).

Title: Enroll an MDN via CSV, then verify Welcome/Remind One/Remind Two SMS delivered IDs match Titan DB and contract ID appears in Titan OpenSearch Dashboard.
Action:
1. Create a new SMS contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=true, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique values.
2. Create the CSV file containing this contract/MDN and upload it to the S3 bucket.
3. Wait for the enrollment job to run then wait up to ten minutes for processing to complete.
4. In the carrier enrollment database, run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target mt ORDER BY created_datetime DESC; Locate the row for the test MDN.
5. In the customer contracts database, run: SELECT * FROM customer_contracts WHERE mdn = '<mdn>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Replace <mdn> with the MDN from Step 1. Note the contract_id.
6. In Titan DB, get the SMS log data for this contract (delivered IDs for Welcome, Remind One, Remind Two). Run: SELECT * FROM titan_views.sms_system_message_realtime_view WHERE contract_id = '<contract_id>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Note the SMS delivered IDs.
7. Open the web app and navigate to the Titan Open Search Dashboard application.
8. Log in via SSO.
9. Compare the SMS delivered IDs for Welcome, Remind One, and Remind Two from Step 6 with the logs displayed in Titan OpenSearch. Verify they match.
10. In the Titan OpenSearch Dashboard, enter a query using the Contract Id (from Step 5) in the search text field and execute the search.
11. Verify that the contract id appears in the logs in the Titan OpenSearch Dashboard.
Expected Result:
1. Contract data ready.
2. CSV created and uploaded.
3. Enrollment completes.
4. Enrollment record visible.
5. Contract ID obtained.
6. SMS delivered IDs obtained from Titan DB.
7. On Titan OpenSearch Dashboard.
8. Logged in.
9. Welcome/Remind1/Remind2 delivered IDs match Titan DB SMS logs.
10. Query executed.
11. Contract ID is present in Titan OpenSearch logs.
Expected Result (Overall): Welcome/Remind One/Remind Two SMS delivered IDs match Titan DB SMS logs, and the contract ID is visible in Titan OpenSearch Dashboard.

Title: Enroll an MDN via CSV, then verify Welcome/Remind One/Remind Two SMS delivered IDs match Titan DB and Enrollment/Welcome SMS logs visible in Horizon OpenSearch Dashboard.
Action:
1. Create a new SMS contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=true, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique values.
2. Create the CSV file containing this contract/MDN and upload it to the S3 bucket.
3. Wait for the enrollment job to run then wait up to ten minutes for processing to complete.
4. In the carrier enrollment database, run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target mt ORDER BY created_datetime DESC; Locate the row for the test MDN.
5. In the customer contracts database, run: SELECT * FROM customer_contracts WHERE mdn = '<mdn>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Replace <mdn> with the MDN from Step 1. Note the contract_id and subscriber_id.
6. In Titan DB, get the SMS delivered IDs for Welcome, Remind One, and Remind Two. Run: SELECT * FROM titan_views.sms_system_message_realtime_view WHERE contract_id = '<contract_id>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10;

Title: Verify Call reservation confirmation SMS is sent after reserving a time slot (unprovisioned MDN, AU).
Action:
1. Create a SMS new contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=false, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique subscriberId and MDN. This represents an unprovisioned MDN for call reservation.
2. Using the system's call reservation flow (UI or API) reserve a time slot with this new MDN for call reservation.
3. Wait 10 seconds (10000 ms).
4. Verify that Call reservation SMS transmission is completed. In ISS DB run: SELECT * FROM sms_system.message ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 100; Identify the Call reservation confirmation SMS for this MDN/contract.
Expected Result:
1. Contract/MDN created.
2. Time slot reserved.
3. Time for SMS to be sent.
4. Call reservation confirmation SMS sent and verifiable.
Expected Result (Overall): Call reservation confirmation SMS transmission is completed.

Title: Verify Call reservation SMS is not sent when the user is provisioned as auNonSubscriber (AU).
Action:
1. Create a SMS new contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=false, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique subscriberId and MDN.
2. Using the project's provisioning process provision the user as new auNonSubscriber for this MDN.
3. Using the system's call reservation flow reserve a time slot with this new MDN for call reservation.
4. Wait 35 seconds (35000 ms).
5. Verify that Call reservation SMS is not sent to this provisioned user. In ISS DB run: SELECT * FROM sms_system.message ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 100; Confirm there is no Call reservation SMS for this MDN/contract.
Expected Result:
1. Contract/MDN created.
2. User provisioned as auNonSubscriber.
3. Time slot reserved.
4. Time for SMS window.
5. Call reservation SMS is not sent to the provisioned user.
Expected Result (Overall): Call reservation SMS is not sent to the provisioned user.

Title: With Manual delivery ON (isNormalOperationMode=false), enroll via CSV, verify SMS in TO_BE_PROCESSED, approve re-delivery, then verify DELIVERED in Titan and Welcome in ISS.
Action:
1. Call the isNormalOperationMode endpoint and set the value to "false" in the JSON body.
2. Verify that manual delivery mode of SMS is ON when isNormalOperationMode is "false" (per project verification step).
3. Create a SMS new contract with: subscriberId (e.g. dynamic_Subscriber), MDN (e.g. dynamic_MDN), serviceEnabled=true, kfsServiceEnabled=true, appleSubscriptionEnabled=true, birthday=19700101, gender=female, brand=AU. Use unique values.
4. Create the CSV file containing this contract/MDN and upload it to the S3 bucket.
5. Wait for the enrollment job to run then wait up to ten minutes for processing to complete.
6. In the carrier enrollment database, run: SELECT * FROM carrier_integration.message_target mt ORDER BY created_datetime DESC; Locate the row for the test MDN.
7. In the customer contracts database, run: SELECT * FROM customer_contracts WHERE mdn = '<mdn>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 10; Replace <mdn> with the MDN from Step 3. Note the contract_id.
8. Wait 40 seconds (40000 ms).
9. In Titan DB, for case "1", verify status SUCCESS for the enrolled contract. Run: SELECT contract_id, status, created_datetime FROM <titan_contract_status_table> WHERE contract_id = '<contract_id>' ORDER BY created_datetime DESC;
10. In ISS DB, verify that Welcome, Remind One, and Remind Two are not delivered yet (manual mode). Run: SELECT * FROM sms_system.message ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 100;
11. In Titan DB, verify that all three SMS delivered logs have status TO_BE_PROCESSED. Use the project's Titan SMS delivery status table or view.
12. Check for duplicate logs in Titan DB (run the project's duplicate-check query for Titan).
13. Check for duplicate logs using a GET request to the issMock endpoint (use the project's URL and parameters).
14. Send a POST request to the endpoint that approves re-delivery so that all three SMS delivered status will be updated and reflected in ISS DB.
15. Wait 40 seconds (40000 ms).
16. In Titan DB, verify that all three SMS delivered logs have status DELIVERED. Use the same Titan SMS delivery status table or view as in Step 11.
17. Wait 10 seconds (10000 ms).
18. In ISS DB, verify the Welcome message is delivered to the newly subscribed user. Run: SELECT * FROM sms_system.message ORDER BY created_datetime DESC LIMIT 100; Identify the Welcome message for the contract from Step 7.
19. Call the isNormalOperationMode endpoint and set the value to "true" in the JSON body to restore normal operation.
Expected Result:
1. Request accepted; Manual delivery ON.
2. Manual delivery mode = ON.
3. Contract data ready.
4. CSV created and uploaded.
5. Enrollment completes.
6. Enrollment record visible.
7. Contract ID visible.
8. Processing time.
9. Status = SUCCESS.
10. No Welcome/Remind1/Remind2 in ISS.
11. All three SMS in TO_BE_PROCESSED.
12. No duplicate logs.
13. No duplicates in issMock.
14. Re-delivery approved.
15. Processing time.
16. All three SMS = DELIVERED.
17. Processing time.
18. Welcome message visible in ISS DB.
19. Normal operation restored.
Expected Result (Overall): With Manual delivery ON: SMS in TO_BE_PROCESSED; after re-delivery approval, all three show DELIVERED in Titan and Welcome is delivered in ISS. No duplicate logs; isNormalOperationMode set back to true.
